name: My City Personal Loan's
description_short: My City Personal Loans is a free loan marketplace that connects consumers with third-party lenders offering personal loans up to $5,000, specializing in applicants with low credit scores.
description_long:
My City Personal Loans operates as a loan matching service based in Los Angeles, California, rather than as a direct lender. The company functions as an intermediary platform designed to connect consumers seeking personal loans with a network of third-party lenders and lending partners. According to their website, they explicitly state they do not make loans, do not make credit decisions, and do not charge fees for their service.

The platform offers connections to several loan product types including personal loans, bad credit loans, and unsecured loans, with advertised loan amounts up to $5,000. They emphasize a simple online application process with instant responses and promote fast approval timelines with potential next-business-day funding. The service targets consumers across various credit profiles, with particular emphasis on those with low or poor credit scores who may struggle to qualify through traditional lenders.

My City Personal Loans distinguishes itself through several claimed advantages: a free service model with no hidden fees, emphasis on consumer privacy, friendly customer service, fast turnaround times, and willingness to work with borrowers of all credit types. They highlight 256-bit SSL encryption and TLS security best practices for data protection. The company operates from a physical address in Los Angeles (3660 Gillig Ave) and maintains a phone line for customer contact.

A critical caveat is that My City Personal Loans is a lead generation platform, not a lender. Consumers should understand that submitting their information means data will be shared with third-party lenders and marketers. The company disclaims responsibility for lender actions and explicitly notes that some connected lenders may be tribal lenders operating under different regulatory frameworks. Loan approval, terms, APRs, and actual funding depend entirely on individual lender criteria, and the 'fast funding' claims are conditional on final approval and documentation review.
pros: Free service with no fees charged to consumers for the matching platform
Serves applicants with low or poor credit scores where traditional lenders may decline
Simple online application form with stated instant response capability
Potential next-business-day funding from connected lenders
Physical office location in Los Angeles with phone support (323-870-6833)
Advertised 256-bit SSL encryption and TLS security best practices
No pre-payment penalties on most connected lender loans
Soft credit inquiries available on some loan products
cons: Not a direct lender—this is a lead generation platform that shares consumer data with multiple third-party lenders and marketers
Some connected lenders are tribal lenders subject to different regulatory frameworks with potentially higher APRs and fewer consumer protections
Actual loan terms, APR, and approval depend on individual lender criteria—no guarantee of approval or specific rates
The company does not have access to fee and charge information from connected lenders, so consumers cannot comparison shop before submission
Funding timelines are conditional on final approval and receipt of all documentation; advertised 'next day' is not guaranteed
best_for: Consumers with low or poor credit scores who have been declined by traditional lenders
Borrowers seeking personal loans up to $5,000 who value application simplicity
Those prioritizing fast application turnaround and potential quick funding
Applicants willing to accept data sharing with multiple lenders in exchange for wider matching options
diagnosis: My City Personal Loans is best for consumers with poor credit seeking quick access to small personal loans ($5,000 or less) who understand they are submitting data to a marketplace of multiple lenders rather than applying to a single institution. The primary caveat is that actual approval, rates, terms, and funding depend entirely on third-party lender criteria, and consumers should be aware that some connected lenders may be tribal entities with fewer regulatory protections and potentially predatory terms.
services: Loan marketplace matching service connecting consumers to third-party lenders
Free online loan request form submission
Soft credit inquiries on select loan products
Consumer data sharing with network lenders for evaluation
Physical office location services in Los Angeles
